jokes.text: Oh, Lordy Lou! Help!
jokes.setup: Professor is in some kind of distress from his experiment
jokes.punchline: His panicked religious exclamation using folksy language
jokes.explanation: Classic Farnsworth panic with his characteristic old-timey exclamations

jokes.text: I know you've rejected me a lot before, but frankly... I wasn't sure we were right for each other either. But now I am!
jokes.setup: Fry asking Leela out despite repeated rejections
jokes.punchline: His backwards logic that mutual uncertainty somehow makes him more confident
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - taking rejection as encouragement shows his endearing cluelessness

jokes.text: I think I left my toaster on. / Buddha! Zeus! God! One of you guys, do something!
jokes.setup: Leela gives a lame excuse to avoid dating Fry
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth desperately appealing to multiple gods simultaneously while in crisis
jokes.explanation: Farnsworth's interfaith panic prayer while Leela makes excuses creates great comedic juxtaposition

jokes.text: Help, Satan! You owe me!
jokes.setup: Farnsworth already asked multiple gods for help
jokes.punchline: He escalates to asking Satan and implies he has a debt to collect
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that implies Farnsworth's dark past dealings with Satan. The casual 'you owe me' is perfectly delivered

jokes.text: I can't go out tonight... because I have sweaty boot rash! / No spluh.
jokes.setup: Leela needs another excuse to avoid dating Fry
jokes.punchline: She gives a deliberately gross medical excuse, Fry responds with made-up slang
jokes.explanation: Decent gross-out humor with Fry's nonsensical 'No spluh' response

jokes.text: Why do you think I'm sitting over here in the stink-free zone?
jokes.setup: Leela claimed to have sweaty boot rash
jokes.punchline: Fry reveals he's been sitting far away because he could smell it
jokes.explanation: Good callback that makes Leela's excuse backfire - he believes it and it's working against her

jokes.text: Good news, everyone! I'm still technically alive, yes.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth's signature 'Good news everyone' opening
jokes.punchline: The 'good news' is barely surviving and he qualifies it with 'technically'
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of his catchphrase - the 'good news' is pathetic survival with dark humor

jokes.text: I could hit it with a shovel. / That's not good enough.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth explains the box needs the sun's energy to be destroyed
jokes.punchline: Bender suggests the comically inadequate solution of hitting it with a shovel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - offering violent but completely inadequate solutions to complex problems

jokes.text: This one time I pounded a guy into the ground like a stake with a shovel.
jokes.setup: Bender defending his shovel suggestion
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of extreme violence with a construction metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender casual violence with a vivid and disturbing image delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Ow! You hurt my collator! / I don't care.
jokes.setup: Bender gets hurt trying to peek in the box
jokes.punchline: He complains about damage to his 'collator' and Farnsworth's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Bender's technical complaint and Farnsworth's cold dismissal

jokes.text: Forbidden! Pretty tantalizing, though.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth warns everyone not to look in the box
jokes.punchline: He immediately admits it sounds tempting
jokes.explanation: Good Pandora's Box reference with Farnsworth undermining his own warning

jokes.text: Whatever's in there, it's the only thing I've ever wanted.
jokes.setup: The forbidden box creates curiosity
jokes.punchline: Amy declares it's everything she's wanted without knowing what it is
jokes.explanation: Captures human psychology of wanting the forbidden, delivered with Amy's typical impulsiveness

jokes.text: In my experience boxes are usually empty... or maybe with a little cheese stuck to the top. And one time, pepperoni. What a day that was!
jokes.setup: Discussion about the mysterious box contents
jokes.punchline: Fry's mundane box experiences culminating in nostalgic pepperoni memory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry - turning cosmic mystery into mundane pizza box memories with genuine nostalgia for finding pepperoni

jokes.text: Fry, how can you be so naive? He was joking, get it? / No. / That's what makes it so funny!
jokes.setup: Bender claims Farnsworth was joking about the box warning
jokes.punchline: Fry doesn't get it and Bender says that's what makes it funny, then admits he wasn't joking
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about joke explanations with Bender's faulty logic and immediate correction

jokes.text: Okay, he wasn't joking. Now shut up and follow me.
jokes.setup: Bender just claimed the professor was joking
jokes.punchline: He immediately reverses position when it suits him
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment showing his self-serving inconsistency

jokes.text: Ow! It's hot! The butter in my pocket is melting!
jokes.setup: They're presumably near something hot (the sun)
jokes.punchline: Fry's concern about pocket butter melting
jokes.explanation: Absurdist gold - who carries pocket butter? Classic Fry priorities in crisis situations

jokes.text: Tangled-up Christmas lights! We can take shifts untangling them! / And unlabeled booze! Wide-mouth too!
jokes.setup: Bender has given them decoy items while he guards the real box
jokes.punchline: Their enthusiasm for terrible gifts - tangled lights and mystery alcohol
jokes.explanation: Great character moments - Amy's enthusiasm for tedious tasks, Bender's appreciation for sketchy alcohol

jokes.text: And unlabeled booze! Wide-mouth too!
jokes.setup: Finding more junk in Bender's decoy box
jokes.punchline: Fry gets excited about mystery alcohol, noting the bottle opening size
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Fry's enthusiasm for questionable alcohol and focus on practical details

jokes.text: A whole evening of TV, gone! What a mockery of justice that I can't take even a little tiny peek.
jokes.setup: Leela has dutifully watched the box all night
jokes.punchline: She frames missing TV as a 'mockery of justice' while considering breaking her duty
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Leela's internal conflict with dramatic language about missing TV

jokes.text: Okay, heads I look, tails I don't. / Oh, yeah. Oh, yeah! Heads! I mean, all right, then.
jokes.setup: Leela decides to flip a coin to make the decision
jokes.punchline: Her excited reaction to getting 'heads' reveals she wanted to look all along
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Leela's excitement betraying her true desires despite the moral framework

jokes.text: Well, then again, I promised the coin I would.
jokes.setup: Leela is conflicted about looking after the coin flip
jokes.punchline: She anthropomorphizes the coin to justify her decision
jokes.explanation: Brilliant rationalization - treating a coin flip as a binding contract with the coin itself

jokes.text: Ouch! My lawyer will hear about this!
jokes.setup: Leela falls into the box
jokes.punchline: Her first concern is threatening legal action
jokes.explanation: Modern litigious society joke with Leela's practical response to supernatural events

jokes.text: I meant your hair. It's all different-y.
jokes.setup: Fry asks 'What have you done?' and Leela assumes he knows about the box
jokes.punchline: He was just commenting on her hair with his typical made-up word
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Fry's obliviousness and his characteristic non-word 'different-y'

jokes.text: Leela? Oh, there's a woman for you. Always dyeing her hair instead of not looking in a box.
jokes.setup: Alternate Leela appears with different hair
jokes.punchline: Bender's sexist generalization about women's priorities gets the situation backwards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - casual misogyny while completely misunderstanding the situation

jokes.text: Clone? Robot? Or long-lost twin? Taking all bets! I also offer video poker!
jokes.setup: Everyone's confused about the duplicate Leela
jokes.punchline: Bender sees it as a gambling opportunity and randomly plugs video poker
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender - monetizing confusion and randomly advertising unrelated services

jokes.text: It's some guy wearing a Leela costume! Get him!
jokes.setup: Everyone is trying to figure out the two Leelas situation
jokes.punchline: Fry's conclusion that it must be a man in a costume
jokes.explanation: Good Fry logic - his mind immediately goes to the most mundane explanation

jokes.text: Am not! / It contains a parallel universe!
jokes.setup: Farnsworth says Fry is being as dumb as usual
jokes.punchline: Fry's childish denial followed immediately by Farnsworth's cosmic revelation
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of Fry's childishness with the universe-altering revelation

jokes.text: Now look, I am not evil. My loan officer said so.
jokes.setup: Accusations that parallel universe people are evil
jokes.punchline: Using a loan officer as a character reference for not being evil
jokes.explanation: Absurdist comedy gold - loan officers are usually seen as morally dubious themselves

jokes.text: Or should I say, 'Evilla'?
jokes.setup: Bender suspects the alternate Leela is evil
jokes.punchline: His terrible pun combining 'evil' and 'Leela'
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that's intentionally bad, fitting Bender's style but not particularly funny

jokes.text: This is awful! Somewhere, there's a more evil Bender than me! I do my best, damn it!
jokes.setup: Discussion of evil twins in parallel universes
jokes.punchline: Bender is offended that someone might be more evil than him and takes pride in his evil efforts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - being competitive about evil and treating it like a professional skill he works hard at

jokes.text: I tell you, they're not evil. But don't be confused. They are jerks.
jokes.setup: Defending her universe from evil accusations
jokes.punchline: Admits they're jerks while denying they're evil, as if this is better
jokes.explanation: Good distinction between evil and jerk with the implication that being jerks is somehow acceptable

jokes.text: Oh, wow! It's like that drug trip I saw in that movie... when I was on that drug trip.
jokes.setup: Fry sees the parallel universe
jokes.punchline: His confused layered reference to drug trips within drug trips
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry confusion with meta-layers - experiencing something that reminded him of a movie he watched while high

jokes.text: Baldercrap! I created your universe!
jokes.setup: Farnsworth claims he created the parallel universe
jokes.punchline: Alternate Farnsworth's colorful dismissal using a made-up curse word
jokes.explanation: Good invented curse word that sounds like Farnsworth while being family-friendly

jokes.text: Baldercrap! I created your universe! / All you created was my fist parallel to your face!
jokes.setup: Two Farnsworths arguing over who created which universe
jokes.punchline: Made-up swear word followed by geometry-based threat
jokes.explanation: Good Farnsworth language with 'Baldercrap' and the mathematical description of punching

jokes.text: We're exactly the same. I know all her moves. Therefore, I've got the upper hand.
jokes.setup: The two Leelas are fighting
jokes.punchline: Leela's flawed logic that knowing her opponent's moves gives her an advantage when they're identical
jokes.explanation: Good logical paradox - if they're identical, both would have the same advantage

jokes.text: Now, now, perfectly symmetrical violence never solved anything.
jokes.setup: Two identical characters fighting
jokes.punchline: Mathematical/scientific description of mirror-image fighting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line - scientific precision applied to violence with the implication that asymmetrical violence might work

jokes.text: Hey! Why can't we be Universe A? / We want A! / It's the best letter!
jokes.setup: Trying to label the universes A and B
jokes.punchline: Petty argument over letter designation as if it matters
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about meaningless status competition over arbitrary labels

jokes.text: All right, you can be crummy Universe A. And we'll be Universe One.
jokes.setup: Resolving the universe naming dispute
jokes.punchline: Leela's passive-aggressive concession that still claims superiority
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's competitive nature and need to win

jokes.text: Or the Mongooses. That's a cool team name. 'The Fighting Mongooses.'
jokes.setup: Argument about universe names continues
jokes.punchline: Random suggestion of 'Mongooses' as a team name with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist suggestion with Bender's typical enthusiasm for arbitrary cool-sounding things

jokes.text: Interesting. Apparently the key difference between our universes... is that coin flips have opposite outcomes.
jokes.setup: Comparing how the two Leelas made different decisions
jokes.punchline: Reducing infinite cosmic possibilities to coin flip outcomes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant scientific observation that reduces parallel universe complexity to simple probability

jokes.text: That explains fruity here. I tossed a coin to pick my finish. / Fog hat gray. / Hey! Bite my glorious golden ass!
jokes.setup: Coin flip theory explains differences
jokes.punchline: Bender's insulting name for his alternate self and the color reveal with signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good callback with variation - 'glorious golden' instead of 'shiny metal' - and Bender's immediate hostility to himself

jokes.text: Hey! Bite my glorious golden ass!
jokes.setup: Regular Bender calls alternate Bender 'fruity'
jokes.punchline: Alternate Bender's variation on his classic catchphrase emphasizing his gold coloring
jokes.explanation: Good variation on the classic Bender line that incorporates his visual difference

jokes.text: Nonsense! I would never do such a thing. Unless you were already having been going to do that!
jokes.setup: Accused of planning to destroy the other universe
jokes.punchline: His convoluted temporal grammar while making a threat
jokes.explanation: Good Farnsworth moment with intentionally confusing time-travel grammar

jokes.text: You guys are dating? / Oh, no, no. We're married.
jokes.setup: Leela asks if Fry and alternate Leela are dating
jokes.punchline: The correction that they're married - much more serious than dating
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing how different the universes are - what's impossible in one is routine in the other

jokes.text: Good point. What do you say we just hit a strip joint? / I was waiting for one of us to say that!
jokes.setup: The two Benders realize they can't watch each other effectively
jokes.punchline: Immediate agreement to abandon duty for strip clubs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - immediately abandoning responsibility for hedonistic pursuits

jokes.text: Bender A, you're a prince among robots. Can you forgive me for distrusting you?
jokes.setup: The Benders have bonded over their shared vice interests
jokes.punchline: Alternate Bender's overly formal and dramatic apology
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's occasional grandiose speech patterns

jokes.text: I'm not imaginary, I'm parallel.
jokes.setup: Amy thinks alternate Amy is an imaginary friend
jokes.punchline: The correction with scientific precision
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay correcting the misunderstanding with scientific terminology

jokes.text: Is that pink nail polish?
jokes.setup: Fry notices something about his alternate self
jokes.punchline: He focuses on the nail polish detail
jokes.explanation: Simple observational setup for the next exchange

jokes.text: I am not evil!
jokes.setup: Amy calls her parallel self evil over nail polish
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic denial of evil based on cosmetic choices
jokes.explanation: Weak joke - dramatic denial over trivial difference doesn't land strongly

jokes.text: I'd make up stuff like, 'I have sweaty boot rash'... or 'I have to meet the president.'
jokes.setup: Alternate Fry describing Leela's excuses
jokes.punchline: References earlier joke and escalates to meeting the president
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows parallel excuses with escalation to more ridiculous ones

jokes.text: Oh, man, you never heard such excuses. But, like a dope, I believed her.
jokes.setup: Alt-Leela listing her ridiculous rejection excuses
jokes.punchline: Alt-Fry admits he fell for obviously false excuses and calls himself a dope
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware character moment - Alt-Fry recognizing his own gullibility

jokes.text: I flipped a coin. / You mean you flipped a coin too? And it was tails? / So that's why you said you had to meet that ghost!
jokes.setup: Alternate Leela explains using coin flip for dating decision
jokes.punchline: Reveals Leela's excuse involved ghosts due to tails result
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that connects the coin flip theory to specific absurd excuse

jokes.text: So that's why you said you had to meet that ghost!
jokes.setup: Learning about the alternate universe's different coin flip outcome
jokes.punchline: Fry realizes his Leela's ghost excuse from the parallel revelation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that connects the parallel universe logic to their own experience

jokes.text: One year later I gave Leela a diamond scrunchy, and we were married.
jokes.setup: Describing his successful relationship
jokes.punchline: The romantic gesture was a 'diamond scrunchy' - an absurd luxury item
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist luxury item - 'diamond scrunchy' perfectly combines expensive materials with mundane hair accessory

jokes.text: One year later I got beat up at a Neil Diamond concert... by a guy named Scrunchy!
jokes.setup: Parallel universe Fry gave Leela a diamond scrunchy and got married
jokes.punchline: Regular Fry's pathetic parallel experience with specific absurd details
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy - the specific misfortunes are perfectly pathetic and memorable

jokes.text: Oh, this old thing? I was experimenting to see if I could remove my own brain.
jokes.setup: Asked about his head wound
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth casually describes horrific self-experimentation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Farnsworth moment - casual tone about horrific self-experimentation shows his dangerous scientific curiosity

jokes.text: Oh, this old thing? I was experimenting to see if I could remove my own brain.
jokes.setup: Question about Farnsworth's head wound
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of self-brain surgery as 'this old thing'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Farnsworth - treating dangerous self-experimentation casually with scientific curiosity

jokes.text: Well, getting the brain out was the easy part. The hard part was getting the brain out.
jokes.setup: Explaining the brain removal experiment
jokes.punchline: Contradictory statement that both parts were getting the brain out
jokes.explanation: Absurdist logic that makes no sense but feels like confused scientific explanation

jokes.text: Well, don't look into it, but I'm a respected internal-medicine doctor.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg describing himself
jokes.punchline: He tells them not to look into his claim of being a respected doctor
jokes.explanation: Great alternate Zoidberg moment - the 'don't look into it' suggests he's lying about his success

jokes.text: As for me, I design mansions, then live in them. / I'm lying. I'm an appalling failure!
jokes.setup: Alternate Zoidberg claims success as mansion designer
jokes.punchline: Regular Zoidberg immediately admits to lying about success
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - fantasy followed by honest self-deprecation with dramatic language

jokes.text: I'm lying. I'm an appalling failure!
jokes.setup: Just described his successful life
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he was lying about everything
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that shows even alternate universe Zoidberg is still a failure

jokes.text: They're all like, 'Stop spraying me with ink, Zoidberg!' 'Put on pants, Zoidberg!' 'Don't touch our fancy box, Zoidberg.'
jokes.setup: Zoidberg complaining about workplace discrimination
jokes.punchline: List of complaints escalating from reasonable (pants) to absurd (ink spraying) to relevant (box touching)
jokes.explanation: Great Zoidberg character work - mixing legitimate workplace issues with his bizarre behavior

jokes.text: Someday they'll watch from down in the gutter, they will... as King Zoidberg caresses their fancy box!
jokes.setup: Both Zoidbergs complaining about mistreatment
jokes.punchline: Grandiose revenge fantasy involving becoming royalty and touching the forbidden box
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg grandiosity combined with petty revenge - 'King Zoidberg' is hilariously specific

jokes.text: Someday they'll watch from down in the gutter, they will... as King Zoidberg caresses their fancy box!
jokes.setup: Zoidbergs plotting revenge
jokes.punchline: Grandiose fantasy of becoming king and touching the forbidden box
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg megalomania with the specific weird desire to caress the box

jokes.text: After carefully reading the scriptures... we've concluded that none of us are evil.
jokes.setup: Determining if the alternate universe people are evil
jokes.punchline: Using religious texts as a scientific method for determining evil
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic showing Farnsworth's mix of science and religion

jokes.text: Your granny can go to hell!
jokes.setup: Hermes quotes his granny's wisdom about doing things yourself
jokes.punchline: Alternate Hermes's harsh dismissal of the dead grandmother
jokes.explanation: Dark humor attacking a dead grandmother figure, shocking but not particularly clever

jokes.text: No. Now, like my granny used to say in her tarpaper shack on Montego Bay:
jokes.setup: Hermes explaining his lack of curiosity
jokes.punchline: He references his grandmother's wisdom from a 'tarpaper shack' in a wealthy resort area
jokes.explanation: Good contrast comedy - poverty (tarpaper shack) in wealthy location (Montego Bay), sets up Hermes' background

jokes.text: Your granny can go to hell!
jokes.setup: Hermes quotes his grandmother's wisdom
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth's aggressive response to the deceased grandmother
jokes.explanation: Good Farnsworth outburst showing his lack of social filters even about the dead

jokes.text: God rest her zombie bones.
jokes.setup: Talking about his deceased grandmother
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to her being a zombie
jokes.explanation: Good dark twist on a traditional blessing phrase

jokes.text: Like Granny said, 'If you want a box hurled into the sun... you got to do it yourself!' God rest her zombie bones.
jokes.setup: Hermes going to destroy the box himself
jokes.punchline: Quotes granny again but reveals she was a zombie
jokes.explanation: Great callback with the zombie revelation that recontextualizes the earlier quote

jokes.text: Now it's my turn, maybe? / The box says no.
jokes.setup: Zoidbergs with the stolen box
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg anthropomorphizes the box to deny sharing
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg selfishness using the box as an excuse not to share

jokes.text: Item one: box. Check. Item two: sun. That's a big check.
jokes.setup: Hermes approaching the sun to destroy the box
jokes.punchline: His bureaucratic checklist approach to universe destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes - treating cosmic destruction like routine paperwork with his characteristic thoroughness

jokes.text: Hmm. All women. I'll put this one aside for later.
jokes.setup: Creating test universes to find theirs
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth's lecherous comment about the all-female universe
jokes.explanation: Mild dirty old man humor that's predictable for Farnsworth's character

jokes.text: Plus, this box is stuck on my fat head!
jokes.setup: Hermes is trying to help find their universe
jokes.punchline: Admits to both the physical problem and self-insults
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with self-deprecating humor about being fat

jokes.text: Yes, it's the apocalypse, all right. I always thought I'd have a hand in it.
jokes.setup: Recognizing their universe is ending
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth's matter-of-fact acknowledgment that he expected to cause the apocalypse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Farnsworth's self-awareness about his dangerous scientific work

jokes.text: I'm not sad, because I finally found someone as great as me.
jokes.setup: Facing the end of the universe
jokes.punchline: Bender's narcissistic silver lining about meeting his alternate self
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - even facing doom, he's focused on his own greatness

jokes.text: It's like I always say, make new friends, but keep the old. / One is silver-- / And the other's gold.
jokes.setup: Bender bonding with his alternate self
jokes.punchline: Bender quotes a wholesome friendship saying and they sing it together
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Bender quoting wholesome friendship wisdom while being selfish and destructive

jokes.text: We didn't see anything. Ever.
jokes.setup: Looking for Zoidbergs in cowboy universe
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic denial that extends beyond the current question
jokes.explanation: Good visual universe gag with the dramatic western-style denial

jokes.text: Hell, no! / Shut up! / Beat it, jerk!
jokes.setup: Visiting the big-headed universe
jokes.punchline: Same personalities but with big heads being more aggressive
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag with personality exaggeration, mildly amusing

jokes.text: Negative. Will you go out with me? / Access denied.
jokes.setup: Robot universe versions being asked about Zoidbergs
jokes.punchline: Robot Bender asks out robot Leela using computer terminology for rejection
jokes.explanation: Good robot universe gag with computer terminology for dating rejection

jokes.text: Access denied.
jokes.setup: Robot Bender asks Robot Leela out
jokes.punchline: Her computer-like rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect robotic version of Leela's usual rejection of advances

jokes.text: Yeah, Leprechaun Universe is fine, if you haven't seen Pirate Universe!
jokes.setup: Bender excited about leprechaun universe
jokes.punchline: Alternate Bender one-ups him with pirate universe
jokes.explanation: Good competitive one-upmanship between Benders about themed universes

jokes.text: Baby, they're somewhere. Everything's, like, somewhere. Place is kind of au naturel right now.
jokes.setup: Asked about boxes in their universe
jokes.punchline: Philosophical hippie response about the nature of existence
jokes.explanation: Good hippie characterization with pseudo-philosophical babble

jokes.text: Baby, they're somewhere. Everything's, like, somewhere. Place is kind of au naturel right now.
jokes.setup: Looking for boxes in hippie universe
jokes.punchline: Hippie philosophy about everything being somewhere delivered in characteristic style
jokes.explanation: Good hippie universe characterization with vague philosophical non-answers

jokes.text: Dig it! All of you fitting in this box is, like, seriously freaked up!
jokes.setup: Seeing multiple people come out of a small box
jokes.punchline: Hippie's amazement at the impossible physics
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to the absurd physics of the situation

jokes.text: Dude, there's a universe in all of us. / Right on, Professor Freaksworth!
jokes.setup: Professor explaining universe theory in hippie style
jokes.punchline: Hippie version of Professor Farnsworth's name and agreement
jokes.explanation: Good character name variation with hippie philosophy about internal universes

jokes.text: Okay.
jokes.setup: Dramatic plea for Hermes not to press the button that destroys universe
jokes.punchline: His casual, understated agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - massive buildup resolved with casual compliance

jokes.text: So, Leela? Seeing how the universe wasn't destroyed... you wanna catch an ape fight? You know, together?
jokes.setup: Crisis resolved, Fry tries asking Leela out again
jokes.punchline: His romantic proposition involves ape fighting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry - treating ape fighting as a romantic date activity with awkward phrasing

jokes.text: You know, let's just say it's heads.
jokes.setup: Leela about to flip coin for date decision
jokes.punchline: She decides to skip the flip and just say yes
jokes.explanation: Sweet resolution to the coin flip theme - choosing to make her own decision rather than leaving it to chance

jokes.text: Sweet honeybee of infinity!
jokes.setup: Realizing they now possess their own universe in a box
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth's characteristic strange exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Farnsworth exclamation that's both absurd and oddly poetic - perfectly captures the cosmic absurdity

jokes.text: Bender, quit destroying the universe!
jokes.setup: Bender is shaking the box containing their universe
jokes.punchline: Casual command about an incredibly serious action
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement treating universe destruction as a minor annoyance

jokes.text: And the box itself is probably worth something too.
jokes.setup: Philosophical discussion about cherishing the universe
jokes.punchline: Bender reduces cosmic significance to monetary value
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - completely missing the profound meaning and focusing on material value
